title: Leptospira Isolation from Animal Samples Protocol
author: Gomard Yann
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.ifccbiw

steps.title: Following euthanasia, place one kidney in...
steps.instruction: Following euthanasia, place one kidney in a sterile microtube containing 1.0 mL of EMJH+AFAS+5-FU. This microtube can be stored at room temperature until the end of animal dissection

steps.title: Transfer the freshly dissected kidney into 1
steps.instruction: Transfer the freshly dissected kidney into 1.5 mL tube containing 1.0 mL of ethanol 70 % for 30 seconds in order to remove potential contaminations present at the surface of the organ

steps.title: Transfer the kidney to a microtube containing 1
steps.instruction: Transfer the kidney to a microtube containing 1.0 mL of sterile water for 30 – 60 seconds in order to remove ethanol

steps.title: Transfer the kidney on a sterile...
steps.instruction: Transfer the kidney on a sterile petri dish and, using a sterile scalpel, cut a fine transversal slice (1 mm) of the kidney. For more comfort you can use a sterile tip to maintain the kidney during the process

steps.title: Within this fine slice, cut a...
steps.instruction: Within this fine slice, cut a small piece containing a part of the cortex, the medulla and the renal pelvis

steps.title: Crush this small piece using a disposable scalpel
steps.instruction: Crush this small piece using a disposable scalpel. This step will last from 1 to 5 min (the longer, the better). Add 100 - 200 µL of EMJH+AFAS+5-FU on biological material in order to facilitate this slicing step. The goal here is to slice the material as finely as possible

steps.title: With a sterile 1000 µL filtered...
steps.instruction: With a sterile 1000 µL filtered tip, suck the mixture containing finely sliced kidney tissue into a microtube containing 1.0 mL EMJH+AFAS+5-FU (final volume, 1-1.2 mL). The remaining kidney tissue can be conserved in a sterile microtube at -20 °C or -80 °C, or in EtOH 70% (room temperature) for subsequent experiments such as DNA extraction for Leptospira PCR detection

steps.title: Keep the microtube containing sliced tissue...
steps.instruction: Keep the microtube containing sliced tissue at room temperature for 45 – 60 min (avoid direct sunlight). Mix by inversion each 15 min. We think that this step is important as it may allow leptospires to “swim” out of kidney tissues

steps.title: Discard tips as well as disposable...
steps.instruction: Discard tips as well as disposable scalpel and petri dish in a proper laboratory trash container

steps.title: Store the three inoculated tubes in...
steps.instruction: Store the three inoculated tubes in the incubator at 28 °C. When attempting cultures in the field with little equipment and no power supply, tubes should be maintained in a polystyrene box to limit temperature variations until reaching lab facilities

steps.title: After one week of incubation, for...
steps.instruction: After one week of incubation, for each tube, put a drop of the inoculated medium on a clean microscope slide and visually check the presence of Leptospira under the dark field microscope

steps.title: Repeat the step 12 once a week for four months
steps.instruction: Repeat the step 12 once a week for four months

steps.title: In the case of positive cultures,...
steps.instruction: In the case of positive cultures, perform a sub-culture by transferring 1.0 mL of the positive culture into 3.0 -5.0 mL of fresh medium (i, ii or iii) without 5-FU

steps.title: If some positive cultures present contaminations...
steps.instruction: If some positive cultures present contaminations with other microorganisms, you can try to perform a sub-culture into a fresh liquid medium with 5-FU or alternatively filter the culture by using a sterile syringe and a 0.22 µm filter

steps.title: Isolates should be stored by different means
steps.instruction: Isolates should be stored by different means: - By directly freezing 1.0 mL of the Leptospira culture in a -80 °C freezer and/or in a nitrogen tank. - By inoculating a tube containing 5.0 mL of Fletcher medium +RS 8% (medium iii) (without 5-FU) with 100 - 500 µL of the Leptospira culture, which can be maintained at room temperature for six months

steps.title: After 45 – 60 min, mix...
steps.instruction: After 45 – 60 min, mix the microtube by inversion and pipet 250 µL of the solution into three Falcon ® culture tubes, each tube containing 2.5 mL of one of the three (i, ii and iii) media described under the “reagents” section. For bacterial isolation using urine, inoculate 2 - 4 urine droplets in each of the three culture media. In addition, to limit contamination issues, it is possible to dilute each inoculated tube 10 times (ex: 250 µL of i into 2.5 mL of i), although in our hands contaminations were not much of an issue
